parsed_review.summary: CP00-285 is a descriptor-only RP08 email/DMS pack (risk C, 150 units, RP08.P04.M06.S05-RP08.P05.M03.S08) that closes the RP08.P04 UI phase (M06-M10) and opens the RP08.P05 fixture foundation (M00-M03). Pack identity, unit count, range, and the deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title distributions reconcile exactly across the pack binding (registry.js), review baseline, manifest, and the live closeout-pack-plan.json, and sum to 150. All 7 required public exports exist (service.js, validators.js) and are surfaced via index.js export-*. The CP285 validators enforce coverage drift, descriptor-only/runtime_execution=false flags, the full no-leak guard set, authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false), contract-projection drift, and CP00-286/RP08.P05.M03.S09 handoff routing; the contract validation script deep-equals the contract JSON against freshly computed source constants (strong anti-drift), and model.test.js asserts coverage.valid, foundation.valid, descriptor-only rows, and the authority boundary. The production_ready claim is appropriately conservative: manifest/command-evidence declare production_ready=false with only production_ready_candidate=true derived from local validation, explicitly gated behind human final approval; no IO/runtime imports exist in the package (no fs/http/db/process.env/fetch), all result objects are frozen, no_real_data=true, and Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic evidence/receipt shapes (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false everywhere). Review-hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence covers every CP178+ rejection case (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped, disabled tools, missing references, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims), the readiness auth preflight passed, tools are restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, and post-review evidence files (claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection) are correctly absent because this review gates them. Changed-file scope is contained to the email-dms package plus the contract and validate script with no scope creep, and unrelated dirty files (the .docx and the LDIP invalid-attempt JSON) are correctly excluded. One trivial non-blocking P3: changed-file-scope.json lists the pack's own artifact dir and handoff doc under both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, but active_pack_implementation_refs correctly excludes docs so the implementation review scope is unaffected. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approval authority.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies pack artifact dir as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: The untracked entries 'docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md' and 'docs/closeout-packs/cp00-285/' appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 95-98) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105). These are contradictory buckets: the docs/closeout-packs/cp00-285/ directory holds this pack's own manifest.json and command-evidence.json, so it is pack-related, not unrelated. This is an evidence-hygiene inconsistency only; the authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs list (lines 5-14) correctly contains just the 8 source/contract/test/script files and excludes these doc paths, so the implementation review scope, the descriptor-only boundary, the production_ready gating, and the review-hardening determinations are all unaffected.
findings.recommendation: Align the scope classifier so a path is not emitted into both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved; the cp00-285 artifact directory and the new-session-handoff doc should be treated as pack-adjacent untracked artifacts rather than 'unrelated', or excluded from one bucket. No change to pack deliverables is required.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_evidence_hygiene; does not affect descriptor-only boundary, production_ready gating, no-real-data/no-write claims, or review hardening; safe to close pack and address in scope-generator cleanup.
